rationale: The citing paper uses Ghosh and John (2009) in its conceptual framing, not merely as a passing reference: it cites that work to motivate research on complex channel governance and to support the relevance of brand effects in B2B governance contexts. In the discussion/implications, the authors explicitly position their contribution relative to that 2009 study, stating that their findings similarly expand branding research beyond traditional consumer contexts. While the citing paper does not replicate the cited paper's specific method, the cited paper meaningfully informs how the new study is framed and positioned.
relation_description: The citing study builds on the branding-and-governance research stream represented by Ghosh and John (2009) and extends it to partially integrated downstream channels. It moves from OEM-supplier branded component contract choice to ongoing manufacturer governance decisions (retailer flexibility and sales-force control) under contingent alignment effects.
